2026-04-08T19:34:07.6210118Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-08T19:32:13.926589902Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57944,\"title\":\"[ConfidentialLedger] Docs: Update README with Examples, RedirectPolicy, and Tags snippet coverage\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57869 by `@ryazhang-microsoft`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nCommit d543f21 added URL-caching to `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy` to improve redirect performance for write operations. The policy now caches the latest primary node URL and reuses it for subsequent non-GET requests (CHANGELOG `1.4.1-beta.5`). The `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy` itself was introduced in `1.4.1-beta.3` and fixed in `1.4.1-beta.4`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `## Examples` section in `README.md` only contains the text \\u0026#34;Coming Soon...\\u0026#34; — there are no real examples at all\\n- `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy` is mentioned in the CHANGELOG for beta.3 and beta.5 but never mentioned or described in the README; developers have no way to understand that the SDK automatically follows 307/308 redirects and that write performance is improved through URL caching\\n- The Tags feature code samples in the README use plain `` ```C# `` fences instead of `C# Snippet:` snippet-tool markers, so they are not backed by compiled, test-validated code\\n- `VerifyConnection` defaulting to `true` (fixed in beta.4) is not explained anywhere in the README outside of the CHANGELOG\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/README.md`\\n**Action:** Edit\\n\\n1. Replace the `## Examples` section stub with a real examples section that mirrors the Key Concepts snippets already present (they already have `Snippet:` markers).\\n\\n2. Add a new sub-section under `## Key concepts` (or a standalone `## Redirect behavior` section) describing `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Redirect handling\\n\\nThe `ConfidentialLedgerClient` automatically follows HTTP 307 and 308 redirects while preserving the `Authorization` header. This is necessary because Azure Confidential Ledger is a distributed system that routes write operations to the primary node; non-primary nodes respond with a redirect. The SDK also caches the latest primary node URL obtained from redirect responses and reuses it for subsequent non-GET requests, reducing the number of round-trips for write-heavy workloads.\\n\\nNo additional configuration is required to enable this behavior.\\n```\\n\\n3. Replace the two inline Tags code blocks (which use plain `C#` fences) with properly named snippet placeholders:\\n\\nFor the submit-with-tags block, add a `C# Snippet:CreateLedgerEntryWithTags` placeholder.\\nFor the get-with-tags block, add a `C# Snippet:GetLedgerEntriesWithTags` placeholder.\\n\\n4. Add a note about `VerifyConnection` under the `### Authenticate the client` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** By default, `VerifyConnection` is `true` in `ConfidentialLedgerClientOptions`, which means the SDK validates the ledger\\u0026#39;s TLS certificate against the trusted identity service certificate. Set `VerifyConnection = false` only for development or testing scenarios.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe Tags feature code blocks must be backed by compiled test code. The existing sample files are under `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/tests/samples/`.\\n\\n1. Open or create `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/tests/samples/Sample_Tags.cs`\\n2. Add two methods with the following structure:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// #region Snippet:CreateLedgerEntryWithTags\\nstring tags = \\u0026#34;tag1,tag2\\u0026#34;;\\nResponse result = await Client.CreateLedgerEntryAsync(content, collectionId, tags);\\n// #endregion\\n\\n// #region Snippet:GetLedgerEntriesWithTags\\n// Only one tag is permitted per retrieval operation.\\nvar result = Client.GetLedgerEntriesAsync(collectionId, \\u0026#34;tag1\\u0026#34;);\\n// #endregion\\n```\\n\\n3. In `README.md`, replace the plain C# blocks for Tags with:\\n\\n````markdown\\n```C# Snippet:CreateLedgerEntryWithTags\\n```\\n````\\n\\nand\\n\\n````markdown\\n```C# Snippet:GetLedgerEntriesWithTags\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README currently has these sections:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts\\n3. ❌ Examples — currently says \\u0026#34;Coming Soon…\\u0026#34;; must be populated\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\n**Action for Examples section:** The existing Key Concepts section already contains the primary usage snippets (`AppendToLedger`, `GetStatus`, `GetReceipt`, etc.). The Examples section should link to those or include a summary with a pointer to the `tests/samples/` directory. Alternatively, extract the most common end-to-end flow (create entry → check status → read entry) into an Examples snippet.\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 confidentialledger` — confirms README placeholders are filled from test regions\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 confidentialledger` — no public API changes expected, but run to confirm\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24153862653/agentic_workflow) · ● 341.1K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57944\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-08T19:28:40Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-08T19:28:40Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

The policy now caches the latest primary node URL and reuses it for subsequent non-GET requests (CHANGELOG `1.4.1-beta.5`). The `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy` itself was introduced in `1.4.1-beta.3` and fixed in `1.4.1-beta.4`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `## Examples` section in `README.md` only contains the text \\u0026#34;Coming Soon...\\u0026#34; — there are no real examples at all\\n- `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy` is mentioned in the CHANGELOG for beta.3 and beta.5 but never mentioned or described in the README; developers have no way to understand that the SDK automatically follows 307/308 redirects and that write performance is improved through URL caching\\n- The Tags feature code samples in the README use plain `` ```C# `` fences instead of `C# Snippet:` snippet-tool markers, so they are not backed by compiled, test-validated code\\n- `VerifyConnection` defaulting to `true` (fixed in beta.4) is not explained anywhere in the README outside of the CHANGELOG\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/README.md`\\n**Action:** Edit\\n\\n1. Replace the `## Examples` section stub with a real examples section that mirrors the Key Concepts snippets already present (they already have `Snippet:` markers).\\n\\n2. Add a new sub-section under `## Key concepts` (or a standalone `## Redirect behavior` section) describing `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Redirect handling\\n\\nThe `ConfidentialLedgerClient` automatically follows HTTP 307 and 308 redirects while preserving the `Authorization` header. This is necessary because Azure Confidential Ledger is a distributed system that routes write operations to the primary node; non-primary nodes respond with a redirect. The SDK also caches the latest primary node URL obtained from redirect responses and reuses it for subsequent non-GET requests, reducing the number of round-trips for write-heavy workloads.\\n\\nNo additional configuration is required to enable this behavior.\\n```\\n\\n3. Replace the two inline Tags code blocks (which use plain `C#` fences) with properly named snippet placeholders:\\n\\nFor the submit-with-tags block, add a `C# Snippet:CreateLedgerEntryWithTags` placeholder.\\nFor the get-with-tags block, add a `C# Snippet:GetLedgerEntriesWithTags` placeholder.\\n\\n4. Add a note about `VerifyConnection` under the `### Authenticate the client` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** By default, `VerifyConnection` is `true` in `ConfidentialLedgerClientOptions`, which means the SDK validates the ledger\\u0026#39;s TLS certificate against the trusted identity service certificate. Set `VerifyConnection = false` only for development or testing scenarios.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe Tags feature code blocks must be backed by compiled test code. The existing sample files are under `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/tests/samples/`.\\n\\n1. Open or create `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/tests/samples/Sample_Tags.cs`\\n2. Add two methods with the following structure:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// #region Snippet:CreateLedgerEntryWithTags\\nstring tags = \\u0026#34;tag1,tag2\\u0026#34;;\\nResponse result = await Client.CreateLedgerEntryAsync(content, collectionId, tags);\\n// #endregion\\n\\n// #region Snippet:GetLedgerEntriesWithTags\\n// Only one tag is permitted per retrieval operation.\\nvar result = Client.GetLedgerEntriesAsync(collectionId, \\u0026#34;tag1\\u0026#34;);\\n// #endregion\\n```\\n\\n3. In `README.md`, replace the plain C# blocks for Tags with:\\n\\n````markdown\\n```C# Snippet:CreateLedgerEntryWithTags\\n```\\n````\\n\\nand\\n\\n````markdown\\n```C# Snippet:GetLedgerEntriesWithTags\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README currently has these sections:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts\\n3. ❌ Examples — currently says \\u0026#34;Coming Soon…\\u0026#34;; must be populated\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\n**Action for Examples section:** The existing Key Concepts section already contains the primary usage snippets (`AppendToLedger`, `GetStatus`, `GetReceipt`, etc.). The Examples section should link to those or include a summary with a pointer to the `tests/samples/` directory. Alternatively, extract the most common end-to-end flow (create entry → check status → read entry) into an Examples snippet.\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 confidentialledger` — confirms README placeholders are filled from test regions\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 confidentialledger` — no public API changes expected, but run to confirm\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24153862653/agentic_workflow) · ● 341.1K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57944\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-08T19:28:40Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-08T19:28:40Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

Support complex types in Azure Monitor Query results\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.Monitor.Query\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nAzure Monitor values can have `dynamic` type representing arrays or complex objects of arbitrary depth. However when I query with Azure.Monitor.Query, complex types does not seem to be supported.\\r\\n\\r\\nFor example, I can run the following query on our build pipeline data\\r\\n\\r\\n```kql\\r\\nadx(\\\"[https://azsdkengsys.westus2.kusto.windows.net/Pipelines\\\").Build](https://azsdkengsys.westus2.kusto.windows.net/Pipelines%22).Build) \\r\\n| where QueueTime \\u003e= ago(10d)\\r\\n| where DefinitionName == \\\"python - confidentialledger - tests\\\"                                                   // just some random thing\\r\\n| summarize completed = make_list(BuildId) by OrganizationName, RepositoryId, DefinitionName\\r\\n| take 1\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\nand would get the response like this:\\r\\n\\u003cimg width=\\\"843\\\" alt=\\\"image\\\" src=\\\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/assets/2347409/2fa900c6-df87-4992-af7c-0f3d83658300\\\"\\u003e\\r\\n\\r\\nNow if I run this query with the library\\r\\n\\r\\n```csharp\\r\\nvar res = await client.QueryWorkspaceAsync(\\\"c9d2b89f-64d6-4d4d-a9c5-eb42431124ab\\\",\\r\\n    query,\\r\\n    new QueryTimeRange(DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddDays(-10), DateTimeOffset.UtcNow));\\r\\n\\r\\nConsole.WriteLine(JsonConvert.SerializeObject(res.Value.Table));\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\nI won't get `completed` array\\r\\n\\r\\n```json\\r\\n[[\\\"azure-sdk\\\",\\\"Azure/azure-sdk-for-python\\\",\\\"python - confidentialledger - tests\\\",{}]]\\r\\n```\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\ncomplex values are deserialized\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\ncomplex values are not deserialized\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\n```csharp\\r\\nvar client = new LogsQueryClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\r\\nstring query = @\\\"adx(\\\"\\\"https://azsdkengsys.westus2.kusto.windows.net/Pipelines\\\"\\\").Build \\r\\n| where QueueTime \\u003e= ago(10d)\\r\\n| where DefinitionName == \\\"\\\"python - confidentialledger - tests\\\"\\\"\\r\\n| summarize completed = make_list(BuildId) by OrganizationName, RepositoryId, DefinitionName\\r\\n| take 1\\\";\\r\\n\\r\\nvar res = await client.QueryWorkspaceAsync(\\\"c9d2b89f-64d6-4d4d-a9c5-eb42431124ab\\\",\\r\\n    query,\\r\\n    new QueryTimeRange(DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddDays(-10), DateTimeOffset.UtcNow));\\r\\n\\r\\nConsole.WriteLine(JsonConvert.SerializeObject(res.Value.Table.Rows));\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\nI can also define a model and try to deserialize it\\r\\n\\r\\n```csharp\\r\\nvar res = await client.QueryWorkspaceAsync\\u003cEntry\\u003e(\\\"c9d2b89f-64d6-4d4d-a9c5-eb42431124ab\\\",\\r\\n    query,\\r\\n    new QueryTimeRange(DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddDays(-10), DateTimeOffset.UtcNow));\\r\\n\\r\\nConsole.WriteLine(JsonConvert.SerializeObject(res.Value));\\r\\n\\r\\nclass Entry\\r\\n{\\r\\n    public long IssueNumber { get; set; }\\r\\n    p
